_note: CHINA slab — Shang & early Zhou (c.1600-770 BC), continuing the CHINA island from where 07z_china.json ends (CN_JIE, last Xia king, c.1650 BC). Rendered as a SUCCESSION king-list (not a strict bloodline): each ruler's single 'child' is the next ruler, each paired with a '—' placeholder spouse. The Shang overthrew the Xia, so Tang the Great follows Jie. Dates follow the Xia-Shang-Zhou Chronology Project / received chronology and DECREASE down the chain. Status flagged honestly: EARLY SHANG (Tang, Tai Jia, the early-Shang bridge) is SEMI-LEGENDARY — traditional kings not individually pinned by archaeology; LATE SHANG (Wu Ding onward, incl. Di Xin) is HISTORICALLY ATTESTED by oracle-bone inscriptions; the ZHOU dynasty is HISTORICAL. Bridge nodes compress stretches of kings the chain skips. The Duke of Zhou is noted as a REGENT, not a reigning king (no crown). No fabrication: only figures, dates, and relationships found in the received tradition and the Chronology Project. All ids prefixed CN_ so they classify to the CHINA house; connects to the existing island via CN_JIE.

children.label:
👑 Tang the Great (Cheng Tang)
c.1600 BC · founder of the Shang dynasty; overthrew the tyrant Jie of Xia at the Battle of Mingtiao · EARLY SHANG, SEMI-LEGENDARY

👑 Tai Jia
c.1550 BC · grandson of Tang; exiled then restored to virtue by the sage minister Yi Yin · EARLY SHANG, SEMI-LEGENDARY

👑 the early & middle Shang kings
c.1400 BC · a bridge across many reigns (Tai Wu, Pan Geng & others) as the capital moved to Yin · EARLY SHANG, SEMI-LEGENDARY

👑 Wu Ding
c.1250 BC · the great Shang king (r. c.1250-1192); his reign is the first firmly attested by oracle-bone inscriptions · LATE SHANG, HISTORICAL

👑 the later Shang kings
c.1150 BC · a bridge across the reigns (Zu Jia, Wu Yi & others) documented on the oracle bones · LATE SHANG, HISTORICAL

👑 Di Xin (King Zhou of Shang)
c.1075 BC · last Shang king (r. c.1075-1046); a byword for tyranny, defeated & ended at the Battle of Muye, 1046 BC · LATE SHANG, HISTORICAL

👑 King Wen of Zhou (Ji Chang)
c.1056 BC · the sage who founded the Mandate of Heaven; led Zhou as a state under the late Shang & died before the conquest · ZHOU, HISTORICAL

👑 King Wu of Zhou (Ji Fa)
c.1046 BC · son of Wen; overthrew the Shang at the Battle of Muye to found the Zhou dynasty · ZHOU, HISTORICAL

👑 King Cheng of Zhou
c.1042 BC · young son of Wu; his reign consolidated Zhou rule over the former Shang lands · ZHOU, HISTORICAL

Duke of Zhou (Ji Dan)
c.1041 BC · brother of King Wu; REGENT for the boy-king Cheng, not a reigning king; crushed the Three Guards' revolt & shaped Zhou ritual · ZHOU, HISTORICAL

👑 the early Zhou kings
c.1000 BC · a bridge across Kings Kang & Zhao, the height of Western Zhou power · ZHOU, HISTORICAL

👑 King Mu of Zhou
c.976 BC · long-reigning king (r. c.976-922) famed in legend for his travels to the Queen Mother of the West · ZHOU, HISTORICAL

👑 the middle Zhou kings
c.860 BC · a bridge across Kings Gong through Li & Xuan, as royal authority slowly eroded · ZHOU, HISTORICAL

👑 King You of Zhou
c.781 BC · last Western Zhou king; his folly with Bao Si & the beacon fires led to the sack of Haojing & his death, 771 BC · ZHOU, HISTORICAL

👑 King Ping of Zhou
c.770 BC · son of You; moved the capital east to Luoyi, beginning the Eastern Zhou · ZHOU, HISTORICAL
